prs.title: Adding my name
prs.body:
Before submitting this pull request, check the changes to see it's only the changes you made intentionally
If there are changes to other lines you didn't make deliberately, it's possible that your IDE made the changes with a utility like prettier.
Next time, make sure that you only add your changes by using `git add -p` and rather than `git add Contributors.md`

If you're doing something in the checklist below, put an `x` inside `[ ]` so that `- [ ]` becomes `- [x]`

- [ ] I had fun going through this tutorial (ノ^o^)ノ and learned on the way ٩(＾◡＾)۶
- [ ] There are some things I'd like to improve in this tutorial. I have written them below.
- [ ] There were steps where I had errors while following this tutorial. I have written them below.

prs.title: added public url in a file
Add complete RAG pipeline with Vectorstore, Retrieval Node and UI integration
prs.body:
Description:
This PR adds a complete Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline to the VectoSpace application. The system takes learning gaps identified by the Diagnosis Node, searches a curated educational resource corpus using vector similarity, and returns relevant learning resources with short explanations.

It also integrates the retriever into the LangGraph workflow and introduces new Streamlit UI components to display recommended resources and allow configuration of RAG settings.

Key Changes:

Implemented vectorstore with embeddings and FAISS index for semantic search
Built retrieval engine to fetch relevant resources based on learning gaps
Added LLM-generated summaries explaining why each resource is recommended
Integrated the retriever node into the main LangGraph agent pipeline
Added new Streamlit UI components including RAG controls and a Resources tab

- Same models as Exp 1 & 2: Logistic Regression and Decision Tree
- Tests maximum predictive power of the full dataset
- Includes evaluation metrics: accuracy, classification report, confusion matrix
- Compares results across all 3 experiments in a summary table

prs.title: fixed twoslash custom tags test

labels.name: seems-ai-generated
labels.description: We label this as a rejection to PR that seems to be AI generated. Please do not do that.

prs.body:
The custom-tags test in packages/twoslash/test/rich.test.ts was failing due to unresolved module imports inside the embedded code sample. Since the test is intended to validate Twoslash's custom tag behavior—not TypeScript module resolution—the unresolved imports caused unnecessary TypeScript diagnostics and led to a snapshot mismatch.

This PR adds a // @noErrors directive at the top of the code snippet to instruct Twoslash to ignore TypeScript errors for that block.
This aligns with how other Twoslash tests handle similar cases.

Linked Issues

Fixes #1167

Additional context

The change is minimal (one-line annotation) and does not affect runtime logic.

After adding // @noErrors, the test passes consistently.

No snapshot changes were required except those automatically updated by the test runner.

prs.title: fix(cli): clarify default option in permission prompt
prs.body:
fix(cli): clarify default option in permission prompts

This PR adds “(default: n)” to permission prompts to make it clear that
pressing Enter without typing a response will deny the permission.

Fixes #31463.

### Changes
- Updated `runtime/permissions/prompter.rs` to append “(default: n)” to both
  unary (`[y/n/A]`) and non-unary (`[y/n]`) permission prompts.
- Updated all affected test expectations in `tests/integration/run_tests.rs`
  to reflect the new prompt format.

### Before
┗ Allow? [y/n/A] (y = yes, allow; n = no, deny; A = allow all run permissions) >

### After
┗ Allow? [y/n/A] (y = yes, allow; n = no, deny; A = allow all run permissions) (default: n) >

### Testing
- ✔️ `cargo check -p deno_permissions` passes
- ✔️ All integration tests updated and passing
- ✔️ No behavior changes — only prompt text modified
- ✔️ Default behavior (“deny” on Enter) remains unchanged

### Rationale
Users were previously unsure what happens when pressing Enter at a permission
prompt without selecting an option. This change makes the default action
explicit, improving UX clarity while preserving existing behavior.

prs.title: fix(tsgo): skip CSS imports in host loader to prevent parser panic
prs.body:
This PR fixes a crash in the TypeScript-to-Go (TSGO) host loader when it tries to parse
`.css` files as TypeScript.

TSGO previously attempted to parse CSS imports, which caused parser panics during type
checking. This PR safely ignores CSS files by:

• Adding a helper `is_css_specifier()`
• Skipping CSS in `load_inner()` with an early return
• Skipping CSS in the `readFile` callback
• Adding minimal test files:
   - cli/tests/specs/tsgo/style.css
   - cli/tests/specs/tsgo/css_import_test.ts

The change is minimal, isolated, and affects only TSGO loading behavior.

prs.title: fix(tsgo): skip CSS files to prevent parser panic
prs.body:
Fixes a crash in the TypeScript-Go (TSGO) host loader when encountering `.css` imports.

### Summary
TSGO attempted to parse CSS files as TypeScript, causing a panic. This PR makes the TSGO loader safely ignore `.css` files by returning empty content, matching expected behavior.

### Changes
- Added `is_css_specifier()` helper in `cli/tsc/go.rs`.
- Updated `load_inner` to early-return an empty `LoadResult` for `.css` files.
- Updated `readFile` callback to return an empty string for `.css`.
- Added integration tests under `cli/tests/specs/tsgo/`.


Closes #31423.

prs.title: fix(parser): prevent line increments for U+2028/U+2029 in tokenizer
prs.body:
##  Bug Fix: Correct Line Tracking for `\u2028` / `\u2029` Inside String Escapes

Babel was incorrectly tracking line numbers when a string literal contained
Unicode line terminator escapes (`\u2028`, `\u2029`).  
Although these characters appear inside an escape sequence, the ECMAScript
spec defines them as *actual line terminators* in source text.

### Problem Example

```js
("\\\u2028");
X;

prs.title: Removed space before square brackets in CSS style attribute
prs.body:
Deno’s HTML formatter was incorrectly inserting a space before square brackets in CSS property values inside style attributes. For example:

animation-[value] → animation- [value]


This resulted in invalid CSS syntax and caused issues when using bracket-notation utilities.

This PR adds a post-processing step to preserve valid -[ sequences after malva formats inline CSS. The new fix_css_bracket_spacing() function removes unintended spaces between hyphens and [ characters in CSS property values, ensuring that patterns such as animation-[value] remain correct and unchanged.

Summary of Changes

Added fix_css_bracket_spacing() to normalize bracket syntax after malva’s CSS formatting.

Integrated this fix into the HTML formatting pipeline for inline CSS inside style attributes.

Verified that the example from the issue formats correctly without inserting unwanted spaces.

Confirmed that no other CSS formatting behavior is affected.

Testing & Validation

Added/updated tests to cover this behavior.

cargo test --manifest-path cli/Cargo.toml tools::fmt::test passes.

./tools/format.js runs without producing any diffs.

./tools/lint.js passes.

Checklist

 Descriptive PR title

 References related issue (#30991)

 Tests cover the change

 cargo test passes

 ./tools/format.js passes

 ./tools/lint.js passes

prs.title: Fix @scope variant output and add test for scoped variant
prs.body:
This pull request fixes the output structure for custom variants that use conditional at-rules such as @scope.
Previously, the generated CSS placed the selector before the at-rule, which resulted in invalid nesting. After this change, the compiler correctly emits the conditional at-rule as the outer wrapper, ensuring valid and expected CSS output.
Changes Made:
file variants.scope.test.ts:

Added a new test case verifying that custom variants using @scope produce correctly structured CSS.

Ensures the at-rule wraps the selector and that theme variables are applied properly.
file ast.ts :
 Added a transform in the [optimizeAst]

function that detects when a style rule contains only a single conditional at-rule child (@scope, @media, @supports, @container) and restructures the AST to emit the at-rule as the outer wrapper with the rule inside.

Test Plan:

Verified that the new test case for @scope passes successfully.

Confirmed that all existing test suites continue to pass without regressions.

Validated the generated CSS output structure to ensure correctness and consistency with other conditional at-rules.

Result:

Correctly wraps conditional at-rules like @scope around variant selectors.

Maintains backward compatibility with existing variant behavior.

All tests pass successfully across supported environments.

prs.title: fix: Correctly handle custom variants with @scope
prs.body:
Custom variants defined using an @scope rule were not being generated correctly.
This happened because @scope was not being treated as a conditional at-rule, similar to @media, @supports, and @container.

This change updates the compoundsForSelectors function to include @scope in the list of conditional at-rules, ensuring that it is handled correctly during the variant compilation process.

Additionally, an integration test was added to verify that custom variants with @scope rules produce the expected CSS output, preventing future regressions.
issue
#18961

prs.title: make process.argv a string array instead of getter functions
prs.body:
This PR fixes an inconsistency between Deno’s Node.js compatibility layer and Node.js itself regarding the behavior of process.argv.

In Node.js, process.argv is a string array that contains the absolute path to the Node executable and the path to the executed script as its first two elements.
In Deno (as of v2.5.4), these elements were exposed as getter functions rather than plain strings, which caused incorrect and confusing output when logged directly:

$ deno run main.ts
[ [Getter], [Getter] ]


This patch updates the initialization of process.argv in ext/node/process.ts to assign real string values instead of getter properties:

argv[0] → the current Deno executable path (Deno.execPath())

argv[1] → the resolved main module path (pathFromURL(new URL(Deno.mainModule)))

This makes process.argv fully consistent with Node.js, improving parity and developer experience.

Before
console.log(process.argv)
# Output:
[ [Getter], [Getter] ]

After
console.log(process.argv)
# Output:
[
  "/usr/local/bin/deno",
  "/Users/username/main.ts"
]

prs.title: fix(ui): remove extra right-side padding in terminal when padding set…
prs.body:
When padding: '0 0 0 0' is set in .hyper.js, the terminal still shows a small gap on the right edge.
This happens because .xterm-viewport adds fractional width during fitAddon.fit().

Fix:
After each fitAddon.fit() call, the .xterm-viewport element is corrected by removing its right margin and padding and hiding horizontal overflow.

Result:

Terminal content aligns perfectly with the right edge when padding is 0.
Works across window resize and tiling.
Other padding values continue to work normally.

prs.title: fix(timezone): recompute offset on init to handle DST transitions glo…
prs.body:
This update fixes DST-related inconsistencies in the Day.js timezone plugin.
Previously, timezone offsets were not recalculated when Dayjs instances were reinitialized through methods like .add(), .set(), or .startOf(), causing 1-hour drifts around DST transitions.

Fix:
Patched the private init() method to recompute the timezone offset whenever $x.$timezone is present:

Ensures accurate offsets across all time manipulation methods.

Prevents DST-related time shifts.

Keeps full backward compatibility.

Result:
DST offsets now remain correct for timezone-aware instances across all operations.
All 93 test suites pass successfully.

Linked Issue
https://github.com/iamkun/dayjs/issues/2957

prs.title: fix(timezone): preserve timezone context when adding calendar units
prs.body:
Fix issue where adding days/weeks/months/years to timezone-aware dayjs objects did not preserve timezone context correctly, causing incorrect valueOf() results when converting back to timezone.

The fix overrides the add() method for timezone-aware objects to:
1. Format the date in timezone context
2. Add calendar units without timezone conversion
3. Convert back to timezone with keepLocalTime=true

This ensures all three approaches produce identical results:
- dayjs.tz(time, tz).add(1, 'day')
- dayjs.tz(dayjs.tz(time, tz).add(1, 'day').valueOf(), tz)
- dayjs.tz(dayjs.tz(time, tz).add(1, 'day').startOf('millisecond').valueOf(), tz)

Fixes issue where valueOf() and format() results were inconsistent after adding calendar units to timezone-aware objects.

Linked Issue 
#2957

prs.title: Changed Documentation UI
prs.body:
### Context
The current documentation UI uses an older visual style with strong shadows, high contrast colors, and inconsistent radius tokens.  
This PR delivers a polished CSS-only refresh to align the docs design with modern UI best practices.

### Implementation
- Introduced a **neutral blue–slate palette**
- Established **UI tokens** for radius and elevation
- Improved **typography hierarchy** and legibility
- Simplified **hero layout** and **sidebar design**
- Flattened **button styles** with accessible color contrast
- Enhanced **code block readability**
- Preserved **dark mode** and all accessibility behaviors

### Validation
✅ No functional changes  
✅ Tested across light/dark modes  
✅ Verified reduced-motion settings  
✅ Responsive at all breakpoints

prs.title: Added my story
prs.body:
> # ✨ Open Source Learning Stories – Pull Request Template
> Thank you for contributing to **Open Source Learning Stories**! 🎉 Please ensure your PR follows the guidelines below to help us review it quickly.
> 
> ## ✅ Author Checklist
> > * [x]   I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) guide.
> > * [x]   My story is added inside the **`stories/`** folder.
> > * [x]   The file is named correctly: **`<your-github-username>.md`**. (We recommend using your GitHub username for file naming consistency).
> > * [x]   I used the proper story format (Name, Date, Tools, Experience, Advice).
> > * [x]   I did not edit or delete someone else’s story.
> > * [x]   My contribution is genuine and respectful.
> 
> ## 📖 Description of Changes
> Please provide a clear and brief description of your contribution.
> 
> (e.g., “Added my personal open-source journey story,” “Fixed a typo in the Java section of the README,” etc.)
> 
> ## 🔗 Related Issue (Optional)
> If this Pull Request resolves an existing issue, please link it here.
> 
> * Closes # (issue number)
> 
> ## 🖼️ Screenshots / Preview (Optional)
> If your contribution involves significant text changes or documentation fixes, you can provide a preview here.
> 
> 🎉 **Tip:** Thank you again for being a part of this project! We appreciate your story!

prs.title: Added reset button
prs.body: Adds a “Reset to Original Array” button in the SortingVisualizer component, allowing users to restore the array to its initial unsorted state after sorting. It enhances usability by providing a quick way to return to Step 1, works across all sorting algorithms, and recalculates metrics correctly. #10
